mnemonics: CIA: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability - The core principles of secure distributed systems
keyPoints: A distributed system is a collection of independent computers that appears to its users as a single coherent system.
Key enabling technologies include networking, internet protocols, web browsers, and middleware.
Common distributed system models include client-server, peer-to-peer, and three-tier architectures.
commonMistakes: Assuming distributed systems are the same as parallel systems - distributed systems are physically separate but appear unified, while parallel systems use multiple processors on shared memory.
Confusing virtualization with distributed computing - virtualization creates multiple virtual machines on a single physical server, while distributed computing spans multiple physical machines.
Overlooking network latency and bandwidth limitations when designing distributed applications.
